checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Does parse_deposit_requests correctly filter logs by topic before processing?

reachability.notes: A crafted transaction can cause the deposit contract to emit logs with unexpected topics. If topic filtering is missing, non-deposit events could be processed as deposits.

checklist_item.test_procedure:
1. Locate the parse_deposit_requests function implementation and identify all log processing entry points.
2. Verify that the function checks len(log.topics) > 0 BEFORE accessing log.topics[0].
3. Verify that logs with empty topics array are explicitly skipped without processing.
4. Verify that the comparison log.topics[0] == DEPOSIT_EVENT_SIGNATURE_HASH is performed.
5. Craft a test transaction that emits a log with empty topics and verify it is rejected.
6. Craft a test transaction that emits a log with a non-matching first topic and verify it is rejected.
7. Verify that only logs matching DEPOSIT_EVENT_SIGNATURE_HASH are included in the deposit requests processing.
8. Check for any code paths that could bypass the topic filtering logic.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W6B15-9, Trust Boundary: tb-002, Invariant: SG-002/INV-007, Threat: stride-016

checklist_item.title: Verify Deposit Contract Log Integrity: Can a malicious transaction cause the deposit contract to emit logs that bypass topic filtering?

reachability.notes: A crafted transaction can cause the deposit contract to emit logs with unexpected topics. If topic filtering is missing, non-deposit events could be processed as deposits.

checklist_item.test_procedure:
1. Analyze the deposit contract to identify all event emission paths and their topic structures.
2. Determine if there are any contract functions that can emit logs with zero topics.
3. Determine if there are any contract functions that emit logs with different first topics.
4. Test calling deposit contract functions with edge case inputs to trigger unexpected log emissions.
5. Verify that the EL node correctly handles logs from the deposit contract that are NOT deposit events.
6. Simulate a block containing both valid deposit logs and non-deposit logs and verify only valid deposits are processed.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W6B15-9, Trust Boundary: tb-002, Node: actor-deposit-contract

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Does extract_deposit_data correctly reject deposit event data where length != 576 bytes?

reachability.notes: Attacker can craft transactions that cause the deposit contract to emit events with non-standard data lengths. The length check is the first line of defense.

checklist_item.test_procedure:
1. Locate the extract_deposit_data function and identify where DEPOSIT_EVENT_LENGTH (576 bytes) is defined.
2. Review the length validation logic to confirm it rejects data where len(data) != 576.
3. Craft test inputs with lengths less than 576 bytes (e.g., 0, 1, 575) and verify InvalidBlock is raised.
4. Craft test inputs with lengths greater than 576 bytes (e.g., 577, 1024, max_int) and verify InvalidBlock is raised.
5. Verify that exactly 576 bytes is accepted without raising InvalidBlock.
6. Check for integer overflow/underflow in length calculations that could bypass the check.
7. Ensure error handling does not leak sensitive information about internal state.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W6B15-10, Trust Boundary: tb-002, Assumption: asm-009, Invariant: SG-001/INV-001, Threat: stride-004

checklist_item.title: Verify Deposit Contract Event Handling: Are malformed deposit events from the EL node properly validated at the trust boundary?

reachability.notes: Attacker can craft transactions that cause the deposit contract to emit events with non-standard data lengths. The length check is the first line of defense.

checklist_item.test_procedure:
1. Identify the data flow from actor-deposit-contract through actor-el-node to the deposit data extraction.
2. Review how deposit contract events are received and parsed from the execution layer.
3. Verify that length validation occurs before any parsing or field extraction.
4. Test with truncated deposit data to ensure partial parsing does not occur.
5. Test with oversized deposit data to ensure no buffer overflow or out-of-bounds access.
6. Confirm that InvalidBlock exception properly halts block processing and prevents consensus divergence.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W6B15-10, Trust Boundary: tb-002, Covered Nodes: actor-deposit-contract, actor-el-node

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is bit_reversal_permutation applied BEFORE inverse FFT in polynomial_eval_to_coeff (SG-007)?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Identify the polynomial_eval_to_coeff implementation in the target client. 2. Verify bit_reversal_permutation is called before inverse FFT. 3. Create a test blob and verify coefficient representation matches expected output. 4. Compare coefficients with a reference implementation to detect ordering errors.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W3B4-13, Trust Boundary: tb-003. Omitting or reordering bit-reversal produces incorrect coefficients corrupting all KZG proofs.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Does multiply_polynomialcoeff (SG-009) assert polynomial degree bounds before multiplication?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Locate multiply_polynomialcoeff implementation. 2. Verify assertion: len(a) + len(b) <= FIELD_ELEMENTS_PER_EXT_BLOB. 3. Craft inputs exceeding bounds and verify rejection. 4. Verify product polynomial degree equals deg(a) + deg(b).
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W3B4-15, Trust Boundary: tb-003. Exceeding degree bound produces polynomials outside trusted setup support.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Does interpolate_polynomialcoeff (SG-011) reject duplicate x-values?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Locate interpolate_polynomialcoeff implementation. 2. Verify duplicate x-value rejection or crash on division by zero. 3. Submit batch verification with duplicate cell_indices. 4. Verify duplicate x-values cause rejection before reaching interpolation.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W3B4-18, Trust Boundary: tb-007. Duplicate x-values cause division by zero in weight_adjustment computation.

checklist_item.title: Verify KZG Proof Generation Correctness: Is quotient polynomial Q(X) computed correctly in compute_kzg_proof_multi_impl (SG-014)?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Identify compute_kzg_proof_multi_impl implementation. 2. Verify Q(X) = (f(X) - I(X)) / Z(X) computation. 3. Verify I(X) correctly interpolates f at evaluation points. 4. Verify proof is commitment to Q(X) using KZG_SETUP_G1_MONOMIAL. 5. Compare proof with reference implementation.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W3B4-22, Trust Boundaries: tb-003, tb-004. Incorrect proof generation breaks data availability guarantee.

checklist_item.title: Verify Quotient Polynomial Degree Bounds: Does compute_kzg_proof_multi_impl (SG-014) enforce trusted setup bounds?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Verify quotient degree check: deg(Q) = deg(f) - len(zs). 2. Verify failure when deg(Q) >= len(KZG_SETUP_G1_MONOMIAL). 3. Craft recovery inputs producing high-degree polynomials. 4. Verify proof generation fails explicitly, not silently.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W3B4-23, Trust Boundary: tb-004. Exceeding trusted setup bounds produces invalid commitments.

checklist_item.title: Verify Batch Verification Pairing Equation: Is verify_cell_kzg_proof_batch_impl (SG-015) pairing check correct?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Verify pairing equation: e(LL, LR) == e(RL, [1]). 2. Verify LL = g1_lincomb(proofs, r_powers). 3. Verify LR = KZG_SETUP_G2_MONOMIAL[n]. 4. Verify RL = RLC - RLI + RLP. 5. Attempt to forge proofs and verify rejection. 6. Verify ALL cells in batch are verified.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W3B4-24, Trust Boundaries: tb-005, tb-006, tb-007. Broken verification allows forged data to be accepted.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Does verify_cell_kzg_proof_batch_impl (SG-015) validate commitment_indices?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Verify no duplicate commitment entries in commitment_indices. 2. Verify all commitment_indices[k] < len(commitments). 3. Submit out-of-bounds commitment_indices and verify rejection. 4. Submit duplicate commitments and verify detection.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W3B4-25, Trust Boundary: tb-007. Out-of-bounds access causes crash, duplicates manipulate weight aggregation.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Does coset_shift_for_cell (SG-016) validate cell_index bounds?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Verify assertion: cell_index < CELLS_PER_EXT_BLOB. 2. Submit cell_index >= CELLS_PER_EXT_BLOB and verify rejection. 3. Verify returned value is root of unity from bit-reversal-permuted extended domain.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W3B4-26, Trust Boundary: tb-007. Out-of-bounds cell_index causes memory access beyond roots array.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Does compute_cells (SG-018) validate blob size and produce correct cell count?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Verify assertion: len(blob) == BYTES_PER_BLOB. 2. Submit blob with incorrect size and verify rejection. 3. Verify exactly CELLS_PER_EXT_BLOB cells produced. 4. Verify each cell represents polynomial evaluation over distinct coset.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W3B4-28, Trust Boundaries: tb-001, tb-002. Missing blob length validation allows malformed blobs.

checklist_item.title: Verify Cell-Proof Alignment: Does compute_cells_and_kzg_proofs_polynomialcoeff (SG-019) produce aligned cell-proof pairs?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Verify exactly CELLS_PER_EXT_BLOB cells and proofs produced. 2. Verify each proof is valid for corresponding cell and coset. 3. Use verify_cell_kzg_proof_batch to validate all pairs. 4. Test with various blob inputs to detect misalignment.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W3B4-29, Trust Boundaries: tb-003, tb-004. Cell-proof misalignment causes all sidecars to fail verification.

checklist_item.title: Verify Public Entry Point Validation: Does compute_cells_and_kzg_proofs (SG-020) validate blob size?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Verify assertion: len(blob) == BYTES_PER_BLOB at entry. 2. Submit arbitrary-size blobs and verify rejection. 3. Verify output matches blob_to_polynomial + polynomial_eval_to_coeff + compute_cells_and_kzg_proofs_polynomialcoeff sequence.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W3B4-30, Trust Boundary: tb-001. Public entry point must validate before processing.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Does verify_cell_kzg_proof_batch (SG-021) validate ALL input constraints?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Verify all four input lists have equal length. 2. Verify each commitment has exactly BYTES_PER_COMMITMENT bytes. 3. Verify each cell_index < CELLS_PER_EXT_BLOB. 4. Verify each cell has exactly BYTES_PER_CELL bytes. 5. Verify each proof has exactly BYTES_PER_PROOF bytes. 6. Submit malformed inputs for each constraint and verify rejection.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W3B4-31, Trust Boundary: tb-007. All inputs are untrusted from P2P peers.

checklist_item.title: Verify Commitment Deduplication Correctness: Does verify_cell_kzg_proof_batch (SG-021) preserve semantic equivalence?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Submit verification batch with duplicate commitments. 2. Verify each cell is verified against its original commitment. 3. Compare deduplication results with non-deduplicated verification. 4. Submit duplicate commitments with different cell data and verify correct index mapping.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W3B4-32, Trust Boundary: tb-007. Incorrect deduplication maps cells to wrong commitments.

checklist_item.title: Can the vanishing polynomial construction (SG-022) be falsified with specific missing cell patterns?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Verify vanishing polynomial evaluates to 0 at all missing cell evaluation points. 2. Verify vanishing polynomial is nonzero at all present cell evaluation points. 3. Test coefficient spacing by FIELD_ELEMENTS_PER_CELL. 4. Create various missing cell patterns and verify polynomial correctness.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W3B4-33. Incorrect vanishing polynomial corrupts recovery.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Does recover_polynomialcoeff (SG-023) satisfy algebraic identity?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Verify E(x) * Z(x) == P(x) * Z(x) over FFT domain. 2. Verify division by Z(x) is performed over coset. 3. Submit consistent cells and verify correct recovery. 4. Submit inconsistent cells and verify detection via post-recovery verification.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W3B4-34, Trust Boundaries: tb-008, tb-009. Incorrect recovery produces wrong cells and proofs.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Does recover_polynomialcoeff (SG-023) correctly truncate high-degree coefficients?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Verify truncation to poly_coeff[:FIELD_ELEMENTS_PER_BLOB]. 2. Verify high-degree coefficients are zero for valid recovery. 3. Submit inconsistent cells producing nonzero high-degree coefficients. 4. Verify such recoveries are detected and rejected.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W3B4-35, Trust Boundary: tb-009. Missing truncation validation causes silent data corruption.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Does recover_cells_and_kzg_proofs (SG-024) validate all recovery preconditions?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Verify 50% threshold: require >= CELLS_PER_EXT_BLOB / 2 cells. 2. Submit fewer cells and verify rejection. 3. Verify cell indices are unique, sorted, and < CELLS_PER_EXT_BLOB. 4. Submit duplicate, unsorted, or out-of-bounds indices and verify rejection.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W3B4-36, Trust Boundary: tb-008. Missing precondition checks cause undefined recovery behavior.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Does recover_cells_and_kzg_proofs (SG-024) verify recovered cells against commitment?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Verify post-recovery verification using verify_cell_kzg_proof_batch (SG-021). 2. Submit inconsistent cells from different polynomials. 3. Verify such inputs are detected via post-recovery verification. 4. Verify failed verification rejects the recovery.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W3B4-37, Trust Boundaries: tb-008, tb-009. Missing post-recovery verification allows incorrect data propagation.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Does get_validators_custody_requirement (SG-001/validator) correctly clamp values?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Verify return value in [VALIDATOR_CUSTODY_REQUIREMENT, NUMBER_OF_CUSTODY_GROUPS]. 2. Test with minimum effective_balance and verify minimum clamping. 3. Test with maximum effective_balance and verify maximum clamping. 4. Verify min(max(count, VALIDATOR_CUSTODY_REQUIREMENT), NUMBER_OF_CUSTODY_GROUPS) is correctly applied.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W3B4-38, Trust Boundary: tb-011. Incorrect clamping allows zero custody groups.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Does cells_and_kzg_proofs_construction (SG-002/validator) produce correct cell-proof counts?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Verify exactly CELLS_PER_EXT_BLOB cell-proof pairs per blob. 2. Verify proof slicing: start = i * CELLS_PER_EXT_BLOB, end = (i + 1) * CELLS_PER_EXT_BLOB. 3. Verify cells from compute_cells(blob) align with proofs slice. 4. Submit blob bundle with incorrect proof count and verify detection.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W3B4-39, Trust Boundary: tb-013. Misaligned proofs cause all sidecars to be rejected.

checklist_item.title: Verify Sidecar Construction: Does get_data_column_sidecars (SG-003/validator) produce correct column counts?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Verify exactly NUMBER_OF_COLUMNS sidecars produced. 2. Verify each sidecar.index == column_index. 3. Verify each sidecar contains exactly one cell and one proof per blob. 4. Test with various blob counts to detect column construction errors.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W3B4-40, Trust Boundary: tb-010. Incorrect column indexing causes coverage gaps.
